intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good—the 10x triplet files are there for GSM5746268 (along with two other samples). Now let me load the GSM5746268 sample and run the standard scanpy QC + clustering workflow:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import os

# Set up paths and plotting
DATA_DIR = '/tmp/aba_eval/arm_control/data'
WORK_DIR = '.'
os.makedirs(WORK_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# Load the 10x triplet for GSM5746268
prefix = 'GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1'
matrix_path = f'{DATA_DIR}/{prefix}.matrix.mtx.gz'
barcodes_path = f'{DATA_DIR}/{prefix}.barcodes.tsv.gz'
features_path = f'{DATA_DIR}/{prefix}.features.tsv.gz'

adata = sc.read_mtx(matrix_path)
adata.obs_names = pd.read_csv(barcodes_path, header=None)[0].values
adata.var_names = pd.read_csv(features_path, header=None, sep='\t')[0].values

# Transpose to cells x genes (mtx is genes x cells by default)
adata = adata.T

print(f"Data shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Barcodes (first 5): {list(adata.obs_names[:5])}")
print(f"Features (first 5): {list(adata.var_names[:5])}")
print(f"Data type: {adata.X.dtype}")
print(f"Data density: {adata.X.nnz / (adata.n_obs * adata.n_vars):.4f}")
